name: carbon-txt

metadata.name: carbon-txt
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A command line tool containing a validator for carbon.txt files, by the Green Web Foundation

documentation:
# Carbon.txt validator

This validator reads carbon.txt files, and validates them against a spec defined
on http://carbontxt.org.

# Usage

## With the CLI

Run a validation against a given domain, or file, say if the file is valid TOML,
and it confirms to the carbon.txt spec.

The following commands assume you are working in a virtual environment:

```shell
# parse the carbon.txt file on default paths on some-domain.com
carbon-txt validate domain some-domain.com

# parse a remote file available at https://somedomain.com/path-to-carbon.txt
carbon-txt validate file https://somedomain.com/path-to-carbon.txt

# parse a local file ./path-to-file.com
carbon-txt validate file ./path-to-file.com

# pipe the contents of a file into the file validation command as part of a pipeline
cat ./path-to-file.com | carbontxt validate file
```

### Using UV

If you are not using virtual environments directly, you can use it with
[uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/), which is the recommended way to run the
project.

Add carbon-txt to a project with `uv add`:

```
uv add carbon-txt
```

You can now run the command line tool with `uv run carbon-txt`

### Running outside a project

If you have uv installed, you can run the command line tool like so:

```
# check a file
uv tool run carbon-txt validate ./path/to/file

# run a server
uv tool run carbon-txt serve
```

This will download the latest published version from pypi and run the
corresponding CLI command

## With the HTTP API

You can also validate carbon.txt files sent over an HTTP API.

```shell
# run the carbon-txt validator as a server using the default django server. Not for production
carbon-txt serve
```

For production, [Granian](https://github.com/emmett-framework/granian), a
performant webserver is bundled. Pass the flag `--server granian` to use it.

```shell
# run the carbon-txt validator as a server using the production granian server
carbon-txt serve --server granian
```

classes.name: carbon_txt.exceptions.InsecureKeyException
documentation:
Raised when the default SECRET_KEY is used in a
production django server environment

classes.name: carbon_txt.exceptions.UnreachableCarbonTxtFile
documentation: Raised when we can't reach the carbon.txt file

classes.name: carbon_txt.exceptions.NotParseableTOML
documentation:
Raised when we have a response at a the given carbon txt
URL, but it is not parsable TOML file

classes.name: carbon_txt.schemas.Provider
documentation:
Providers in this context are upstream providers of hosted services.
The domain is used as key for looking up a corresponding provider in the
Green Web Platform

classes.name: carbon_txt.schemas.Credential
documentation:
Credentials are essentially supporting documentation for a claim to be running on
green energy.

classes.name: carbon_txt.schemas.Organisation
documentation:
An Organisation is the entity making the claim to running its infrastructure
on green energy. In the very least it should have some credentials point to, even
if it is exclusively relying on upstream providers for its green claims.

classes.name: carbon_txt.schemas.Upstream
documentation:
Upstream refers to one or more providers of hosted services that the Organisation
is relying on to operate a digital service, like running a website, or application.

classes.name: carbon_txt.schemas.CarbonTxtFile
documentation:
A carbon.txt file is the data structure that acts as an index for supporting evidence
for green claims made by a specific organisation. It is intended to links to
machine readable data or supporting documentation in the public domain.

functions.name: carbon_txt.parsers_toml.log_safely
documentation: Log a message, and append it to a list of logs

classes.name: carbon_txt.parsers_toml.CarbonTxtParser

methods.name: carbon_txt.parsers_toml.CarbonTxtParser.parse_toml
documentation:
Accept a string of TOML and return a dict representing the
keys and values going into a CarbonTxtFile object.

methods.name: carbon_txt.parsers_toml.CarbonTxtParser.validate_as_carbon_txt
documentation:
Accept a parsed TOML object and return a CarbonTxtFile, validating that
necessary keys are present and values are of the correct type.

Responsible for parsing carbon.txt files, checking
they are valid TOML, and that parsed data structures
have the expected top level keys and values.

functions.name: carbon_txt.validators.log_exception_safely
documentation:
Log an an exception, and append it to a list of errors in a form that can
be displayed as JSON.

classes.name: carbon_txt.validators.CarbonTxtValidator

methods.name: carbon_txt.validators.CarbonTxtValidator.validate_contents
documentation:
Validate the provided contents of a carbon.txt file. Returns a CarbonTxtFile object,
or raises a list of validation exceptions if the contents are invalid.

methods.name: carbon_txt.validators.CarbonTxtValidator.validate_url
documentation: Validate a carbon.txt file at a given URL.

methods.name: carbon_txt.validators.CarbonTxtValidator.validate_domain
documentation:
Validate a carbon.txt file at a given domain.
Returns a dictionary containing the CarbonTxtFile,
a list of logs, and a list of exceptions.

The core class repsonsible for exposing essentially the same functionality to
the package's API and CLI.interfaces.

modules.name: carbon_txt.web.manage
documentation: Django's command-line utility for administrative tasks.
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: carbon_txt.web.manage.main
documentation: Run administrative tasks.

functions.name: carbon_txt.finders.log_safely
documentation: Log a message, and append it to a list of logs

classes.name: carbon_txt.finders.FileFinder

methods.name: carbon_txt.finders.FileFinder._parse_uri
documentation: Return a parsed URI object if the URI is valid, otherwise return None

methods.name: carbon_txt.finders.FileFinder._lookup_dns
documentation:
Try a DNS TXT record lookup for the given domain,
returning the delegated carbon.txt URI if found

methods.name: carbon_txt.finders.FileFinder._check_for_via_delegation
documentation: Check for a 'via' header in the response, and return the URL in the 'via' header if present

methods.name: carbon_txt.finders.FileFinder.fetch_carbon_txt_file
documentation:
Accept a URI and either fetch the file over HTTP(S), or read the local file.
Return a string of contents of the remote carbon.txt file, or the local file.

methods.name: carbon_txt.finders.FileFinder.resolve_domain
documentation:
Accepts a domain, and returns a URI to fetch a carbon.txt file from.

Follows delegation logic from DNS TXT records, and the 'via' header in
HTTP responses to resolve to a final URL for a carbon.txt file.

methods.name: carbon_txt.finders.FileFinder.resolve_uri
documentation:
Accept a URI pointing to a carbon.txt file, and return the final
resolved URI, following any 'via' referrers or similar.

This is a recursive function, so it expects to be called, following redirections until
it either arrives at a 200 OK response, or a non 400/500.

This does NOT try to fetch the file, just resolve the final URI to fetch it from

Responsible for figuring out which URI to fetch
a carbon.txt file from.

functions.name: carbon_txt.cli.schema
documentation: Generate a JSON Schema representation of a carbon.txt file for validation

modules.name: carbon_txt.web.config.asgi
documentation:
ASGI config for config project.

It exposes the ASGI callable as a module-level variable named `application`.

For more information on this file, see
<https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/5.1/howto/deployment/asgi/>

modules.name: carbon_txt.web.config.urls
documentation:
URL configuration for config project.

The `urlpatterns` list routes URLs to views. For more information please see:
    https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/5.1/topics/http/urls/
Examples:
Function views
    1. Add an import:  from my_app import views
    2. Add a URL to urlpatterns:  path('', views.home, name='home')
Class-based views
    1. Add an import:  from other_app.views import Home
    2. Add a URL to urlpatterns:  path('', Home.as_view(), name='home')
Including another URLconf
    1. Import the include() function: from django.urls import include, path
    2. Add a URL to urlpatterns:  path('blog/', include('blog.urls'))

classes.name: carbon_txt.web.middleware.AddTrailingSlashMiddleware
documentation:
Middleware to add a trailing slash to specific API endpoints, but
keep the other endpoints like /api/docs and api/openapi.json as they are.

For more, see the links below
https://github.com/vitalik/django-ninja/issues/1058
https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/5.1/topics/http/middleware/

functions.name: carbon_txt.web.api.validate_contents
documentation:
Endpoint to validate the contents of a carbon.txt file.

Args:
    request: The request object.
    CarbonTextSubmission: The schema containing the text contents of the carbon.txt file.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary containing the success status and either the validated data or errors.

functions.name: carbon_txt.web.api.validate_url
documentation:
Endpoint to validate a carbon.txt file at the provided URL.

Args:
    request: The request object.
    CarbonTextSubmission: The schema containing the text contents of the carbon.txt file.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary containing the success status and either the validated data or errors.

functions.name: carbon_txt.web.api.get_json_schema
documentation: Endpoint to get the JSON schema for a carbon.txt file.

classes.name: carbon_txt.web.api.CarbonTextSubmission
documentation: Schema for the submission of carbon.txt file contents. We expect a string, containing the contents of the carbon.txt file.

classes.name: carbon_txt.web.api.CarbonTextUrlSubmission
documentation: Schema for the submission of a URL pointing to a carbon.txt file. We expect an URL pointing to a carbon.txt file, which is downloaded and parsed.
